ops.title: Incident: lock-version poisoning red yclaw's Guides check ŌĆö root cause + fixes (v0.1.37/v0.1.38)
ops.body:
## What happened (2026-07-18/19)

yasyf-home ran a stale cc-guides **0.1.29**. Its unscoped `render` aborted on yclaw's `.claude/fragments/.claude/capt-hook.toml` (TOML support ships in Ōēź0.1.30), so the session there did a **scoped** render of `.pre-commit-config.yaml` instead ŌĆö and any render rewrites the shared lock wholesale, so yclaw commit `2f4a125` silently downgraded `cc-guides.lock`'s `version = "0.1.34"` ŌåÆ `"0.1.29"`. The Guides check action installs **exactly** the lock-recorded version, so every subsequent push installed 0.1.29 and exited 2 on the `.toml` target (yclaw runs 29673179492, 29673302905). The session then greened CI by reverting its own *correct* ruff bump (`5720f58`), misdiagnosing "a pre-existing .toml render bug"; the cc-notes record it cited (`b0307ec4`) was never synced and is absent from yclaw's store.

## Heal

yclaw `ea492f9`: ruff v0.15.20 re-applied + full render with 0.1.36 (lock healed, fresh cc-skills pins) ŌĆö Guides check green.

## Fixes shipped here

- **v0.1.37** (`c31e06a`): version-skew guard ŌĆö `render`/`check` refuse when the binary's release version is older than the lock's (strict X.Y.Z compare; explicit non-empty `--lock-version` is the operator bypass; render refuses writing dev/git-describe pseudo-versions over a clean lock; unparseable lock versions stay heal-able). `lockDiffPinsOnly` now lands `version =` movements and `commit = "local"` repairs instead of skip-reverting them, so the daily re-render self-heals poisoned locks. Supported-extension diagnostics derive from the kind registry.
- **v0.1.38** (`482aec3`): action.yml check-failure hint is skew-aware (generic diagnosis always first; validated latest-release comparison adds the skew hint) and no longer tells operators to "run cc-guides render locally" ŌĆö the act that caused the poisoning. `action-v1` advanced `fe6b3ad` ŌåÆ `482aec3` (`action-v1.2.0` pinned).
- Review: codex finder (9 findings) + adversarial refuter (2, incl. a demonstrated `--lock-version ""` guard bypass) ŌĆö all fixed before ship.

## Outstanding

- **yasyf-home must `brew upgrade cc-guides`** (0.1.29 ŌåÆ Ōēź0.1.38). Until then that machine cannot render repos with newer locks ŌĆö but post-guard it fails loudly with the upgrade message instead of poisoning.
- Doc drift: AGENTS.md ┬¦ Releases says releases are tag-driven; the Release workflow actually auto-cuts a release on every main push (observed: v0.1.37/v0.1.38 auto-tagged).
